mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.25% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 320, the Fed maintains restrictive monetary policy. This sustained high real rates compress equity valuations and push 10-year Treasury yields upward as market participants price in prolonged rate persistence. The mechanism is always-on given current snapshot values.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS > 4.25% and CPIAUCSL > 320, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced inflation pressure, prompting Fed rate cuts. Lower expected future rates compress long-term Treasury yields downward as investors reprice growth and monetary accommodation expectations.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or negative correlation between UNRATE changes and yield changes persists below -0.15 over rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal aggregate demand strength. When both series decline simultaneously by more than 2% month-over-month, it indicates demand weakness that reduces inflation expectations and long-term Treasury yields fall as the market reprices growth and monetary accommodation.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% MoM simultaneously
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of triggered instances, or joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative yield moves with Rank IC < 0.08 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 104.5 (current snapshot 103.99) signals robust supply-side expansion and productivity gains. Strong supply growth reduces inflation pressures and allows the Fed to maintain accommodative policy, pushing 10-year Treasury yields downward as real rate expectations decline.
regime_hint: productivity-driven expansion with disinflationary supply dynamics
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of instances where INDPRO > 104.5, or Rank IC between INDPRO and yield changes falls below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100k per month while UNRATE remains stable, signaling hidden labor market softness. This divergence indicates the Fed will cut rates preemptively, compressing long-term Treasury yields downward as forward rate expectations shift lower.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100k while UNRATE stays within 0.2pp of prior month
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market deceleration with lagging unemployment response
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal (PAYEMS deceleration + stable UNRATE) fails to predict negative yield moves with Rank IC < 0.06 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling economic slowdown. Slower growth reduces inflation expectations and prompts Fed rate cuts, pushing 10-year Treasury yields downward as the market reprices terminal rate expectations and growth outlook.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth annualized rate falls below 2.0%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 annualized growth < 2.0%, or Rank IC between GDPC1 growth and yield changes remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0% and CPIAUCSL momentum (3-month change) turns negative, the Fed enters a restrictive-but-disinflationary regime. Long-term Treasury yields rise as the market prices in extended high rates to combat inflation, with yields reflecting both current restrictive policy and expectations of sustained tightness.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month change turns negative
regime_hint: disinflationary monetary tightening with policy persistence
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines in > 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction signal (high FEDFUNDS + negative inflation momentum) fails to predict positive yield moves with Rank IC < 0.07 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1,200k units while retail sales (RSAFS) remain flat or decline, indicating synchronized demand collapse. This dual weakness signals recession risk, prompting sharp Fed rate cuts and a flight-to-quality bid in long-duration Treasuries, pushing 10-year yields downward sharply.
regime_hint: demand-shock recession scenario with synchronized contraction
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal (HOUST < 1200k + flat RSAFS) fails to predict negative 5-day yield moves with Rank IC < 0.10 over any 12-month window.
